Live Dungeons & Dragons with comedians! Tess Dion, the game master, will guide comedians through a one-shot. This one-shot takes place in Cambridge, that is home to a hidden realm of magic, where the extraordinary coexists with the ordinary. Come and experience this live game, and perhaps, give (polite or funny) advice or just enjoy the afternoon watching the highs, lows and see how they avoid certain death with a beverage and snack in your hand! Disclaimer: Please note that, while this is mostly a kid-friendly show, there will be some swearing and other PG-13 content.

events.title: The Eastern Phoebes
events.description: The Eastern Phoebes formed in the summer of 2025 after meeting at the Passim School of Music. The trio connected over a shared love of tight harmonies and long music sessions in cozy living rooms, with always-full mugs of tea. Drawing inspiration from a mix of genres, they perform covers of folk, Americana, and country tunes, adding their own voicing to the songs of Dolly Parton, The Wailin’ Jenny’s, I’m With Her, and others. The group includes Jen on fiddle, Lily on guitar and banjo, and Zoe on guitar, piano, and melodica — with singing shared by all three.

events.title: TCS presents: Eddie Pepitone
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating. Doors at 5pm.




Eddie Pepitone
 is a master of the dark art of comedy. Hailed as a modern day cross between Jackie Gleason, Don Rickles and Eckhart Tolle. Eddie is a force of nature on stage, switching between social rage and self-doubt. His shows are an energetic combo of calm and chaos, blue-collar angst and sardonic enlightenment. Few comedians working today channel the power of the rant better than Eddie Pepitone.




Eddie's latest stand-up special, 
The Collapse
,
 is out now on streaming platform VEEPS and YouTube. The special is a true Pepitone rant and romp as he barrels through the heightened emotions and anxieties of modern life in a somehow joyful and jovial energy that only Pepitone can deliver. In addition to his regular stand-up shows, Pepitone is a stand-out comedic character actor. Beginning in New York City’s improv and sketch scene where he quickly became a regular face, and continuing on to recurring roles on Late Night with Conan O’Brien and appearances on WTF with Marc Maron Podcast. Most recently, Eddie can be seen in Max's Haley Quinn spin-off Kite Man, Hell Yea!, Hulu's Ramy, and Nickelodeon’s Rock Paper Scissors. Eddie has also appeared on numerous comedy TV shows, including Crazy Ex-Girlfriend, Rick and Morty, Reno 911!, 10 Year Old Tom, The Life and Times of Tim, Will & Grace, It’s Always Sunny in Philadelphia, Love, The Sarah Silverman Program, Jimmy Kimmel Live, After Midnight, Last Comic Standing, Chappelle’s Show, appearances on Bob’s Burgers, and he’s a regular on Adult Swim’s Your Pretty Face is Going to Hell. Fans also might recognize Pepitone from his role in the hit comedy Old School, where he plays the role of 'Archer', one of the recruiting pledges.


Host: 
Andrew Balestieri

events.title: Elan Mehler Trio
events.description:
$15 admission / $10 students / 7pm  / seated 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Elan Mehler  has released 11 albums as a leader, with such musicians as Bill Frisell, Dave Douglas, Becca Stevens, Scott Robinson, Francisco Mela and many more and has garnered stellar reviews from such publications as The New York Times, The Guardian, Jazz Times, and Downbeat.  His latest record is a solo piano record released on Sunnyside Records that has garnered significant press.  Elan has brought his projects to clubs and festivals worldwide including Birdland, Queen Elizabeth Hall, and the North Sea Festival.  In 2016 Elan co-founded the label Newvelle Records, as artistic director of the label he has signed and produced over 50 records by artists such as Jack DeJohnette, John Patitucci, Dave Liebman, Lionel Loueke, Irma Thomas and Ellis Marsalis.  Reviews and music available at  www.elanmehler.com Max Ridley  is one of the most in-demand bassists in the Boston area. He was awarded full-tuition scholarships to study at Berklee College of Music for both undergraduate and graduate degrees, earning his graduate degree from the extremely exclusive Berklee Global Jazz Institute under the direction of master pianist, educator and humanitarian Danilo Perez. An equally important part of his education was down the street from Berklee at Boston’s historic Wally’s Jazz Cafe. He began going to jam sessions there in high school and eventually became the house bass player every Friday and Saturday night. Ridley has played and recorded with renowned musicians of many different styles all over the world.  An incredibly versatile multi-instrumentalist, Max performs regularly in bands playing Jazz, Rock, Folk, Soul, Punk, Klezmer, Polka and every other style you can think of.  He also serves as the music director for tap dance company Subject:Matter ( https://www.subjectmattertap.com/ ) and teaches music as a guest lecturer at different colleges around New England (such as Longy School of Music, University of New Hampshire and Berklee College of Music). Boston based Israeli drummer  Dor Herskovits  is already a staple of the Boston jazz scene and has won international acclaim as an improviser. His melodic approach to playing the drums, combined with vast knowledge of rhythm and a composer's vision make him a unique voice as a drummer. Dor's original music reflects the eclectic nature of music nowadays,  drawing from jazz and blues roots, folk and world music, free jazz rock and metal.   As a sought after sideman, he plays in various jazz and experimental groups on the Boston-New York line. He has also toured around the US, Canada, Europe and China. Herskovits has recorded many albums as a sideman, featured on labels such as Newvelle and Endectomorph music, alongside musicians such Grammy award-winning guitarist Bill Frisell, Michael Blake and Jason Palmer. He has also performed with George Garzone, Noah Preminger, Jon Cowherd, Steve Cardenas,Bert Seager’s Tet-rap-tych, Max Ridley’s Basement Philharmonic, Daniel Rotem and Xiongguan Zhang. As co-leader of Earprint, he received critical acclaim from publications such as Downbeat magazine, NPR’s critics' poll and many more.  www.dorherskovits.com 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Every Monday at 7PM Line-up subject to change

events.title: Jerry Bergonzi Quartet
events.description:
$15 @ the door / $10 students / 8:30 start / 8pm doors / seated  Jerry Bergonzi , Tenor Saxophone Luther Gray , Drums Special Guest , Trumpet Special Guest , Bass 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 A fine, high-powered tenor saxophonist with a tone influenced by John Coltrane, a mastery of chord changes, and a strong musical imagination,  Jerry Bergonzi  has long had an underground following in the Boston area. He started on clarinet when he was eight, switching to alto at 12, and finally to tenor two years later. Bergonzi was inspired early on by Sonny Rollins, Coltrane, and Hank Mobley. He attended Lowell University and then after graduation played electric bass in local bands behind singers and strippers, saving up enough money to move to New York in 1972. After struggling in the Big Apple for seven years and gaining some recognition as a member of Two Generations of Brubeck and of the Dave Brubeck Quartet (with whom he appeared on several Concord albums during 1979-1981), Bergonzi moved back to Boston in 1981, where he developed a strong career both as a tenorman and as an educator. He has since led several groups (including two called Con Brio and Gonz) and recorded for the Plug, Not Fat, Red, and Blue Note labels. —  By Scott Yanow, AllMusic 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Continuing Every Monday at 8:30pm Lineup subject to change

events.title: The Fringe
events.description:
$15 @ the door / $10 students / 10pm doors / 10:30 start / seated  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 The Fringe  originally formed back in 1971 by tenor-saxophonist  George Garzone , bassist Richard Appleman, and drummer Bob Gullotti as an outlet for their free jazz improvisations, The Fringe have entertained and enlightened Boston area jazz audiences for over 50 years. Bassist Appleman left the band in 1985 to become Chairman of the Bass Department at Berklee College and was replaced by veteran bassist  John Lockwood . Bob Gullotti passed away in 2020 and is remembered as one of the greatest jazz drummers of his generation. Francisco Mela, now on drums,  graciously fills the space Bob left behind. Along with their impressive tenure with The Fringe, Garzone, Lockwood, and Mela are in great demand as session players and have played with many of the greats of contemporary jazz. Along with keeping up with a busy performance schedule, each of them are well-respected educators helping to inspire the jazz musicians of tomorrow. George Garzone , Tenor Saxophone John Lockwood , Bass Francisco Mela , Drums -   - Continuing Every Monday at 10:30pm Lineup subject to change without notice

events.title: Gill Aharon Trio
events.description:
$10 / 8:15 start / 8pm doors / Seated Show 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Inspired by the cartoon music of the 50s and 60s, the drive of Led Zeppelin, the genius and humor of Frank Zappa, the perfection of Bach, the relentlessness of Ligeti, the courage of Medeski, Martin and Wood, the jam of Phish live, the beauty of the Beatles and the immersive nature of music, I am in pursuit of truth through music. “Sometimes I think of us musicians as kids playing in order to learn how to be adults, but we can just keep playing. The game of playing music together is too much fun to make it work. With that in mind, I refuse to write arrangements. I never want my friends and bandmates to feel obliged to do anything. - Gill Aharon Gill Aharon , Piano Jef Charland , Bass Andrew Stern , Guitar Randy Wooten , Drums - Continuing Every Wednesday  Lineup subject to change gillaharontrio.com

events.title: Toad Sunday Revival - Tim Gearan Band
events.description: A beloved fixture in the Boston music scene for over 25 years, Tim Gearan has become a bonafide local music hero. His warm, raspy baritone, jaw-dropping guitar skills and wry, poignant songwriting have graced the bars, pubs, and music halls throughout New England and beyond, creating a loyal cult-like following. Gearan, a New York native, spent ten years touring the globe as a sideman for the great Toni Lynn Washington, as well as collaborating with Grammy-winning singer/guitarist Susan Tedeschi. Finding his own artistic voice, Gearan  has crafted an intoxicating blend of Americana, folk and blues with shades of Randy Newman, Tom Waits, JJ Cale,  Leon Russell and Lowell George. He quickly established himself as a force in the Boston’s resurgent roots music scene, and his Boston area residencies have become a “can’t miss” staple of the city’s nightlife. Gearan has released 7 albums to date, as well as 4 single releases; “While the World Goes By” (2019)  “It’s Not the End of the World"  (2019) “Hey, Hey, Hey” (2020) and “Talk About Heaven” (2020).

events.title: TCS presents: Bobcat Goldthwait
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.




Comedian Bobcat Goldthwait first appeared on the David
Letterman Show at the age of 20 and has an ongoing career as a
writer, director, actor, voice actor, and stand-up. As a comedian,
Bobcat has starred in numerous HBO/Showtime specials,
performed in venues worldwide, and even went on tour as an
opening act for the band Nirvana. Well-known for his roles in
“Scrooged” with Bill Murray and as Zed in the “Police Academy”
franchise, Goldthwait has become a well-respected Award-
Winning Director, too. His directorial works include feature films
World’s Greatest Dad, starring Robin Williams, Willow Creek,
starring Bigfoot, God Bless America, Windy City Heat, Sleeping
Dogs Lie, and the documentaries Call Me Lucky and Joy Ride. In
addition to being a 4x Sundance Alumni, Goldthwait’s films have played internationally,
including the Deauville Film Festival and San Sebastian Film Festival. Bobcat has been
honored with the German Independent Honorary Award at the Oldenburg Film Festival for
his collective works and was awarded “Filmmaker on the Edge” at the Provincetown Film
Festival by his hero John Waters. His TV direction includes his series, Misfits and Monsters,
Jimmy Kimmel Live, Community, Maron, Those Who Can’t, and many more. Goldthwait has
directed numerous standup comedy specials for folks like Patton Oswalt, Marc Maron, Ron
Funches, Kyle Kinane, Cameron Esposito, Mo Willems, Gary Gulman, Hari Kondabolu,
Eugene Mirman, and Pete Davidson to name a few. Bobcat is currently working on several
new film projects and has started his autobiography.

events.title: The Late Night
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum.
Need a nightcap of pure, unadulterated hilarity? The Late Night Show is the definitive late-night comedy experience in Boston, where the jokes are as fresh as the craft cocktails. Featuring a new, uncensored lineup of the city's sharpest stand-up and sketch comics each week, we specialize in the kind of high-energy, no-holds-barred comedy that only comes out after dark. It’s loose, it’s loud, and it’s the perfect way to finish your night with a laugh-induced ab workout.

events.title: Anna May
events.description:
Alternative tragic Americana, invocations for peace & judgment reshaping with bleeding heart stream of consciousness poems. The deep exploration of heartbreak and trauma has been an ongoing quest throughout Anna's work. Her lyrics have been heavily textured by a lonesome whimsicality that finds itself most at home in the vastness of the American West or in landscapes of stark frontiers. Anna's music honors the nomadic spirit with memory, meditation & fresh interpretations of folk music & jazz influence. She honors a hope to enhance connection, humility, healing, and joy among people in the process of parting with typical genre rules. Anna's lyrical sensibility is steeped in wisdom, and layered with evocative musicality and metaphysical embroidery while eschewing the mainstream & parting with platitudes. Anna explores both estrangement and connection while taking cues from artists like Billie Holiday, Shawn Colvin, & Neil Young. Anna has been compared to artists like Jeff Buckley & Aimee Mann. Anna has opened for artists like Kacy Hill, Amythyst Kiah, The Arcadian Wild, People Museum, & Jann Klose.

Anna has performed at wildflower music & arts festival, treefort music festival, Brick and Mortar Music Hall in San Francisco, High Dive in Seattle, the Mint in Los Angeles, Spiderhouse Ballroom in Austin, Rockwood Music Hall in New York City, Larimer Lounge in Denver, PNC Live Studio, & many more. 
Anna has been featured in publications like V13 media, WFMU, Medium, Northern Public Radio, & many more. Anna May was nominated for best Indie song of 2023 by Indie Boulevard Magazine. In 2024, Anna was a featured guest on NPR's Great American Folk Show, on WKU PBS Lost River Sessions (episode to air in 2025) & her song, The Show was chosen as song of the day on KUTX in Austin in 2024. In 2024, Anna was nominated for Best Americana Act at the New England Music Awards & was featured on the WDVX Blue Plate Special.

events.description: 🎸  Sunday Revival: Live Music at the Toad With the Chris Cote Band! 4 PM – 7 PM | Where the Music Comes Home We’re bringing it back—one set at a time. From bluesy ballads to high-energy jams, these are the familiar faces and timeless tunes that made the Toad what it is. Whether you’re a longtime fan or hearing them for the first time, join us for good music, good food, and that classic Toad vibe. This is more than live music—it’s a revival. 🎶  Featuring original Toad bands & local legends  📍  McCarthy’s Toad | Sundays 4–7 PM  🍻 Come early, stay late, bring the crew.

events.title: Orkest
events.description:
Founded in 2022 by Sapphire Skye Toth and Michael Gehman while they earned their Master of Science degrees in Arts Administration at Boston University, Orkest is meant to be the place where New Music meets The Community and The Community meets New Music.

Orkest, a visionary musical venture rooted in the bustling city of Boston, is on a mission to redefine the local music scene. Recognizing the abundance of untapped musical talent, Orkest seeks to bridge the gap between emerging composers and the community, breaking away from classical traditions and creating an inviting space for diverse genres. Departing from conventional models, Orkest utilizes venues to bring people to music, not just vice versa, fostering a casual environment in cafe bars and libraries. With a commitment to equitable treatment of artists, creating opportunities for artists at socioeconomic disadvantages, professional recordings of performances, and a unique pay-what-you-wish model, Orkest is not just a music experience; it's a transformative force shaping how communities engage with and appreciate new music and the artists who create it.

events.description:
The Dave Fox Trio is a Modern Jazz Trio featuring original jazz
compositions and arrangements inspired by artists such as John Coltrane, Joe Henderson, Chick Corea, Steve Swallow, Charles Mingus, Thelonious Monk and Wayne Shorter. The group features internationally acclaimed bassist John Lockwood and innovative Boston area musicians and composers such as guitarist Phil Sargent and saxophonists Nicholas Brust and Bill Jones.

Dave Fox is an accomplished, versatile drummer and is currently a full-time member of The Gravel Project, Dan Rockett and The Guilty Party and
“AS3” along with Andrew Stern and Brad Barrett.

As a sideman for over two decades, he has performed with a diverse group of artists including Jerry Bergonzi, John Lockwood, Josh Smith, Dave Tronzo, Charles Neville, Stan Strickland, The Stompers and Tom Hambridge and The Rattlesnakes to name a few.

Caitlin Peluffo
 is a New York-based stand-up

events.title: TCS presents: Jeff Greenspan: Deviant Acts
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.




Comedian and former BuzzFeed Chief Creative Officer Jeff Greenspan shares behind-the-scenes stories from viral pranks he’s pulled on corporate crooks, privacy invaders, and other modern messes. He took what he learned working in media and used it to mess with the media, hijacking news cycles with stunts so bold even NYC’s mayor had to weigh in. Deviant Acts mixes stand-up that roasts media, advertising, and pop culture with footage from these stunts.


Jeff Greenspan
 is a Brooklyn-born comedian. His stand-up, which draws from real-life misadventures and a past life in media and advertising, has led to an appearance on Netflix, writing for filmmaker Michael Moore, and performances at the Limestone, Red Clay, and Portland Queer Comedy festivals.

events.title: Upstairs Show: ISSA Boston/Northeast Singer Songwriter Showcase
events.description:
Learn more / Buy Tickets
   
 
 
 
 
 Linda Marks - Often compared to Carole King for her heartfelt piano-based songwriting & intimate storytelling, Joni Mitchell for her emotional honesty & lyrical depths, and Norah Jones for her smooth, jazz-influenced phrasing and sooting vocal tone, multi-award winning lifelong singer songwriter Linda Marks' music leaves the listener with a feeling of peace and hope.  Her 15th studio album, The Silence Of The Stars won the 2026 World Entertainment Awards Best Folk Album, and her 16th studio album, Queen Of The Angels is a Finalist for Album of the Year in the 2026 International Singer Songwriter Association (ISSA) Awards. Her 17th studio album, Better Than This will be released in March 2027.  Linda performs regularly in venues large and small, has appeared on many radio and tv shows, and offered over 6 years of a weekly livestream born of the pandemic.  Her music has been covered in the Boston Globe, Boston Magazine and on NPR.  Linda is a Voting Member of the Recording Academy, leader of the ISSA Boston/Northeast Chapter and a Juried Artist for Music For Life.  She holds a degree in Music with honors and distinction from Yale University and an MSM from the Sloan School at MIT. https://www.LindaMarksMusic.com https://www.healingheartpower.com Wanda Metcalf  has been writi ng songs for about 20 years. Mostly folk, they include songs about life, cats and protest songs (more of those lately). She also paints, plays go and collects mushrooms. https://music.apple.com/us/artist/wanda-metcalf/566368439   Elizabeth Burke -  What happens when a conservat ory trained classical violinist turns into an old-time, blues, improvising fiddle player and guitar strumming soulful singer-songwriter? The istening experience becomes a mesmerizing mix of truth revealing songs woven together with thought provoking lyrics, powerful singing, unique melodies and instrumental roots playing. https://elizabethburkemusic.com/   Bernie Drury -  Sta rting in 1976 when I spent nearly a year busking in France music has made my life richer in more ways than I can count.  I enjoy songwriters who move from heartfelt to lighthearted - John Prine, Jim Croce and Lyle Lovett to name a few.  I am currently recording a full length album that will be released in the fall of 2026. https://youtube.com/@berniedrurymusic?si=Kq8qGD-vdpsPU6re   "Dan Tappan  writes lyrical songs steepe d from snippets of life, seasoned with dashes of humor and whimsy, a dusting of philosophy, and an occasional pinch of pathos."  https://dantappanmusic.com/ Eric Be hr,  from Riverside, RI., is the winner of the 2026 Rose Garden Coffee House Performing Songwriter Competition. He writes story songs that explore the lived experiences of folks in a variety of situations, some funny and some very tough. https://ericbehr.bandcamp.com/album/reflections-2 $20 in advance. (+Service  fees applied at checkout), $25 at the door. This Is An All Ages Show Doors are at 11:30am. Showtime is 12pm. Dinner and drinks available at all tables. NO REFUNDS OR EXCHANGES

events.title: Toad Sunday Revival - The Dennis Brennan Band
events.description: Dennis Brennan was born on the wrong side of the tracks in Marlboro, Massachusetts. At age 9 he entered show business as Rumplestiltsken in a grammar school play. In 7th grade he performed “Louie Louie” with a local garage band at the Berlin, Ma. Town Hall and quickly evolved into “the lead singer” That band evolved into “The Paranoids”, his musical outlet throughout his high school years. They opened shows for both The Remains and The Barbarians, garage band legends who are each featured on “Nuggets, original artyfacts from the first psychedelic era” Rhino R2 75486. Following high school. Brennan’s education continued as lead singer in such well regarded outfits as “The Dirty Rye Band”, The West Side Band”, “The Martells”, and “Push Push”. These bands performed in every bar and dive on the east coast. An education indeed. In 1992 he formed the Dennis Brennan Band and started his solo career, quickly releasing two critically acclaimed recordings on Upstart Records, a division of Rounder. In 2000 he released “Rule #1” on ESCA Records which resulted in glowing reviews from The New Yorker, The Chicago Sun Times, The Boston Phoenix as well as other publications nationwide. These recordings showcase Brennans ability “to not just tip toes into genres, he strips naked and plunges right in.” Bill Holmes, AMPLIFIER.

events.title: TCS presents: Sam Miller
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.




Sam Miller
 is a nationally touring comedian from Olympia, Washington. He is 6 foot 6, 360 pounds, has two kids, (one of them is on purpose) he's been married for 15 years, and has been clean and sober for 17 years. Sam likes to make jokes about what it's like to be a sober parent. He also likes to make jokes about what jails like in Yakima. His first album and special, Round Trip, was recorded and filmed under the tutelage of Grammy-winning producer Dan Schlissel, whose indie label, Stand Up! Records, Sam had recently signed a three-album deal with. Round Trip was released October 27th 2023 and immediately went to number one on the iTunes Comedy Charts. It even broke into the top 200 overall charts. Sam is a recurring guest and has been heard by an estimated 2.5 million listeners on the nationally syndicated "Bob & Tom Radio Show." He has written for Newsweek. He was the runner-up in the 2021 Seattle International Comedy Competition and won the Comedy on Trial Competition in 2017. He has headlined multiple major comedy clubs including Sidesplitters in Tampa, Helium in Portland, and The Laugh Factory and Zanies in Chicago. He's also performed with the Addicts Comedy Tour and at NA and AA conventions all over the country. For three consecutive years, he won awards in "The Best of Olympia" issue of the Weekly Volcano.
2016 Best Local Hero / 2017 Best Local Celebrity / 2018 Best Comic Relief

Will Abeles
 is a nationally touring comedian and podcaster known for his superb storytelling and irreverent political and social commentary. Affectionately known as The Pride of Hagerstown, Will has most recently gone viral for his appearances on 
Ask a Fuckboy
 and Now This’s " 
Are You Okay?
 and 
Crosswalk Crush
. Currently, Will is on the “And Nobody Likes Will Abeles 2026 Tour” and has featured in Sketchfest in San Francisco, the Nashville Comedy Festival, and Comedy Fort at Treefort Music Festival. Will got his start in comedy in Boston while attending Emerson College before quickly moving to New York City, where he became a regular at the Stand and New York Comedy Club while balancing a career in television in NYC and LA, working on shows like 
The Late Show with David Letterman
, 
Jimmy Kimmel Live
!, The Academy Awards and Emmy Awards, among many others. Now in Nashville, Will’s reached #1 on the iTunes Comedy Charts with back-to-back comedy albums. His sobriety/travel/comedy podcast

events.performer: Let's Not Die: A Live D&D Comedy Performance
events.description:
All Ages w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. 
Live Dungeons & Dragons with comedians! Tess Dion, the game master, will guide comedians through a one-shot. This one-shot takes place in Cambridge, that is home to a hidden realm of magic, where the extraordinary coexists with the ordinary. Come and experience this live game, and perhaps, give (polite or funny) advice or just enjoy the afternoon watching the highs, lows and see how they avoid certain death with a beverage and snack in your hand! Disclaimer: Please note that, while this is mostly a kid-friendly show, there will be some swearing and other PG-13 content.

events.title: Two Towns Band
events.description:
Influenced by artists such as Tyler Childers, The Lumineers, and Noah Kahan, Two Towns weaves their original songwriting into a unique sound by seamlessly blending folk and pop genres into foot-tapping songs you’ll soon find yourself singing along to. The acoustic duo hails from Somerville, Massachusetts and has played various rooms throughout New England, capturing the attention of audiences through their set of catchy originals strewn amongst an eclectic catalog of fan-favorite covers.

Following the recent of their 2nd album “Covered Up”, Two Towns is projected to have their biggest year yet. After gaining numerous new followers and gaining tens of thousands of streams in just 6 months across all platforms, the band is now entering their 6th year of live performances and currently in production of a new album. Whether it be on the road or in the studio, Two Towns always delivers

events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.




Dan St. Germain
’s special Dance Fatty, Dance is free on YouTube along with his Comedy Central Half Hour. You may have also seen him on This is Not Happening, Conan, Late Night with Jimmy Fallon, This Week at the Comedy Cellar, Adam Ruins Everything, Best Week Ever, @Midnight, Guy Code, The Electric Company, John Oliver’s NY Stand-up show, The Roast of Ric Flair, The Roast of Bruce Prichard, and was the voice of The Yeti for Michael’s Christmas campaign. He’s written for The White House Correspondents’ Dinner, Immoral Compass, Alternatino, The Break with Michelle Wolf, Superior Donuts, The Comedy Central Roast of Rob Lowe, Not Safe with Nikki Glaser, Tru TV’s 10 Things, WWE’s Monday Night Raw, and The Roast of Tom Brady. He was the co-host of the Not Safe with Nikki Glaser Podcast on Comedy Central and My Dumb Friends on All Things Comedy. He’s appeared on WTF with Marc Maron, We Might Be Drunk, Soder, Whiskey Ginger, Stavvy’s World, The Adam Friedland Show, The Bonfire, Ten Percent Happier, Are You Garbage, You Made it Weird, Jim and Sam, Bennington, You Be Tripping, Last Podcast Side Stories, The Honey Dew, and Past Times. He’s performed at the Moontower, Just For Laughs, South by Southwest, and Bumbershoot comedy festivals. He’s currently writing a new cartoon for Fox with Dan Soder and Mike O’Brien, co-writing a movie with Andrew Jay Cohen and co-hosting a brand new UAP podcast called Burbs Bros with Sean Donnelly. Also, dogs love him, and he makes a great sandwich.

events.title: Toad - Late Night Cover Band with ‘Indigo’
events.description: We’re turning up the volume every  Saturday night at Toad  with a  live cover band  that knows how to bring the party. Whether you're a familiar face or dropping by for the first time in a while, it's the perfect way to reconnect and keep your weekend rocking. Expect a night packed with  great music, dancing, and serious craic —from classic crowd-pleasers to unexpected favorites that'll have you singing along. Grab a pint, gather your crew, and join us from  10 PM ‘til late . The tunes are loud, the vibes are high, and the dance floor’s waiting.

events.title: Día de Muertos Festival
events.description: A vibrant community celebration filled with tradition, art, and culture, beginning with a colorful procession.

events.title: Sophia Saleh
events.description: Sophia Saleh is a vocalist and songwriter whose music blends R&B, pop, and soul with heartfelt storytelling and smooth, expressive vocals. A 2025 graduate of Colby College, her recent highlights include serving as the featured singer and “showstopper” for both the opening and closing of the 2025 Intandem Fashion Show, which showcased pieces by Good Balance—a brand featured at New York Fashion Week. She has toured this summer with Majinheartz on the Good Purp Tour, performing their original song, “Lost,” live at venues such as Wonderville in NYC. She has also performed at Club Cumming in NYC, the L.L. Bean flagship in Freeport, and at large-scale events at both Colby and Bowdoin Colleges. Sophia will be performing a mix of original songs from her recent EP and select covers, accompanied by Louis Shriber—a versatile jazz pianist and guitarist with a background in choral conducting and vocal performance. Both are members of the Boston-based band Unmuted.

events.title: Toad Sunday Revival - Tim Gearan Band
events.description: A beloved fixture in the Boston music scene for over 25 years, Tim Gearan has become a bonafide local music hero. His warm, raspy baritone, jaw-dropping guitar skills and wry, poignant songwriting have graced the bars, pubs, and music halls throughout New England and beyond, creating a loyal cult-like following. Gearan, a New York native, spent ten years touring the globe as a sideman for the great Toni Lynn Washington, as well as collaborating with Grammy-winning singer/guitarist Susan Tedeschi. Finding his own artistic voice, Gearan  has crafted an intoxicating blend of Americana, folk and blues with shades of Randy Newman, Tom Waits, JJ Cale,  Leon Russell and Lowell George. He quickly established himself as a force in the Boston’s resurgent roots music scene, and his Boston area residencies have become a “can’t miss” staple of the city’s nightlife. Gearan has released 7 albums to date, as well as 4 single releases; “While the World Goes By” (2019)  “It’s Not the End of the World"  (2019) “Hey, Hey, Hey” (2020) and “Talk About Heaven” (2020).

events.title: TCS presents: Dana Gould
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum.




Gould Gould
 started performing stand-up comedy at the age of 17, three short weeks out of high school. Dana Gould has performed in several HBO specials, as well numerous guest appearances on "Late Night With Conan O'Brien" and "The Late Show with David Letterman". Gould recorded the CD Funhouse, which was called "a comedy CD" by Billboard magazine. As an actor, he appeared on "Seinfeld" and "The Ben Stiller Show". After writing the pilot "World On A String" (with Jay Kogen), Gould co-created MTV's late, lamented, Super Adventure Team (with Rob Cohen). Dana Gould then joined the staff of "The Simpsons", where he squatted for seven years. Stand up remains a big part of his career, and he continues to perform regularly.

events.title: JAZZ NIGHT: Bill Jones Trio
events.description: Bill Jones, a native of Marion, Massachusetts, is a tenor and soprano saxophonist that been a regular on the jazz scene since he moved to Boston in 1999 to study at the New England Conservatory. He is inspired by such greats as Joe Henderson, John Coltrane, Hank Mobley, Wayne Shorter, and Charlie Parker, as well as modern masters like Jerry Bergonzi, George Garzone, Joe Lovano, Joshua Redman, and Mark Turner. Bill regularly plays at venues in and around Boston, such as Wally’s, The Beehive, Monkfish, and Lilypad. He enjoys playing classic and modern jazz standards and his original compositions. He has performed with many revered jazz musicians, such as Bob Gullotti, Leo Genovese, Herb Pomeroy, Jeff Galindo, Tim Ray, Greg Hopkins, and John Lockwood.

events.title: TCS presents: Irene Tu
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. 
Irene Tu
 is a Los Angeles-based stand-up comedian, actor and writer who honed her chops in San Francisco. Her first comedy album WE’RE DONE NOW debuted at #1 on the iTunes comedy charts. Paste Magazine praised Irene’s “quick wit, relaxed stage presence, and hilariously off-the-wall observations” in this album that “ticks all the boxes of a winning introduction, and then some.” One of Netflix’s “Introducing” comics of 2022, her show in 2024 was named one of the funniest by the LA Times. She’s a regular at festivals and clubs around the country and has opened for comics like Taylor Tomlinson, Ronny Chieng, and Patton Oswalt. You may recognize Irene from After Midnight, Hulu, Comedy Central, Netflix is a Joke, Don’t Tell Comedy or her viral stand up clips which have amassed over 30 million views on social media.

events.title: TCS presents: Luke Null
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.
Luke Null
 began performing comedy the moment he picked up a guitar in Chicago. During his stay in the Windy city he developed his signature musical comedy style, blending witty humor with catchy ear-worms. This unique approach garnered the attention of Lorne Michaels and earned him the chance to perform as a cast member on 
Saturday Night Live
. After a year in New York as a featured player on SNL, Luke moved to LA and is now a regular at the Hollywood Improv. He performs standup comedy at clubs and colleges across the country. In 2024, Luke released a live standup special entitled 
"Pretty Songs, Dirty Words"
 through 800 Pound Gorilla Records, now available on YouTube.

events.title: JAZZ NIGHT: Ervin Dhimo Trio
events.description: "The Albanian -born electric bassist and his trio specialize in fierce, soulful, funky workouts on jazz, pop,and original tunes…”-  Kevin Lowenthal, The Boston Globe

events.title: Global Arts Live Presents: JOHN SCOFIELD'S "ELECTROSPECTIVE"
Chuck Clough
events.description:
A Boston area singer/guitarist, Chuck primarily performs solo but also plays out with his band, The Butler Frogs. He has opened for touring bands America, The Wallflowers, and The Gin Blossoms. In 2020, The Butler Frogs released their sole EP, Last Call For Opinions.

He was also a founding member of Northern Point (2005-2010), an acoustic trio from the NY/NJ area that released three albums (November, Northern Point: Live at St. James' Gate, and After The Frost). 

In 2016, Chuck founded and hosted (with musician Ronnie Hirschberg) the award-winning podcast Above The Basement - Boston Music and Conversation, which won a 202o Boston Music Award for Podcast/Radio Show of the Year.  After 200 episodes, Chuck shuttered the podcast and set out on his solo musical endeavor. 

 He currently performs in New England and is always on the lookout for the lost songs that have influenced him over the years.

events.title: Toad Sunday Revival - The Dennis Brennan Band
events.description: Dennis Brennan was born on the wrong side of the tracks in Marlboro, Massachusetts. At age 9 he entered show business as Rumplestiltsken in a grammar school play. In 7th grade he performed “Louie Louie” with a local garage band at the Berlin, Ma. Town Hall and quickly evolved into “the lead singer” That band evolved into “The Paranoids”, his musical outlet throughout his high school years. They opened shows for both The Remains and The Barbarians, garage band legends who are each featured on “Nuggets, original artyfacts from the first psychedelic era” Rhino R2 75486. Following high school. Brennan’s education continued as lead singer in such well regarded outfits as “The Dirty Rye Band”, The West Side Band”, “The Martells”, and “Push Push”. These bands performed in every bar and dive on the east coast. An education indeed. In 1992 he formed the Dennis Brennan Band and started his solo career, quickly releasing two critically acclaimed recordings on Upstart Records, a division of Rounder. In 2000 he released “Rule #1” on ESCA Records which resulted in glowing reviews from The New Yorker, The Chicago Sun Times, The Boston Phoenix as well as other publications nationwide. These recordings showcase Brennans ability “to not just tip toes into genres, he strips naked and plunges right in.” Bill Holmes, AMPLIFIER.

events.title: JAZZ NIGHT: Dave Fox Trio
events.description:
The Dave Fox Trio is a Modern Jazz Trio featuring original jazz
compositions and arrangements inspired by artists such as John Coltrane, Joe Henderson, Chick Corea, Steve Swallow, Charles Mingus, Thelonious Monk and Wayne Shorter. The group features internationally acclaimed bassist John Lockwood and innovative Boston area musicians and composers such as guitarist Phil Sargent and saxophonists Nicholas Brust and Bill Jones.

Dave Fox is an accomplished, versatile drummer and is currently a full-time member of The Gravel Project, Dan Rockett and The Guilty Party and
“AS3” along with Andrew Stern and Brad Barrett.

As a sideman for over two decades, he has performed with a diverse group of artists including Jerry Bergonzi, John Lockwood, Josh Smith, Dave Tronzo, Charles Neville, Stan Strickland, The Stompers and Tom Hambridge and The Rattlesnakes to name a few.

events.title: Seth Adam
events.description: Singer/songwriter Seth Adam writes compelling, genuine songs with honest lyrics. Taking musical cues from artists like Counting Crows, Tom Petty, and Jason Isbell, he creates his own brand of edgy, Americana-informed rock & roll with a socially-conscious message. His latest release, “Fits and Starts and Stops,” is "...a 10-track collection of evocative songs" (Randall Radic, Tattoo.com). Ryan Martin from Jammerzine says, “Seth Adam builds a stage for his voice and lyrics in a true and masterful form. Taking nods from vintage radio and Americana, Seth douses that with the fuel of rock and the penchant for classic alternative. This is a lesson for songwriters.”

events.description: Tyler and the Names are a Cambridge based act who have released several full length albums. They can resemble a cross between Captain Beefheart and Dylan's Rolling Thunder Revue. They are not to be missed.

events.title: Toad Sunday Revival - The Dennis Brennan Band
events.description: Dennis Brennan was born on the wrong side of the tracks in Marlboro, Massachusetts. At age 9 he entered show business as Rumplestiltsken in a grammar school play. In 7th grade he performed “Louie Louie” with a local garage band at the Berlin, Ma. Town Hall and quickly evolved into “the lead singer” That band evolved into “The Paranoids”, his musical outlet throughout his high school years. They opened shows for both The Remains and The Barbarians, garage band legends who are each featured on “Nuggets, original artyfacts from the first psychedelic era” Rhino R2 75486. Following high school. Brennan’s education continued as lead singer in such well regarded outfits as “The Dirty Rye Band”, The West Side Band”, “The Martells”, and “Push Push”. These bands performed in every bar and dive on the east coast. An education indeed. In 1992 he formed the Dennis Brennan Band and started his solo career, quickly releasing two critically acclaimed recordings on Upstart Records, a division of Rounder. In 2000 he released “Rule #1” on ESCA Records which resulted in glowing reviews from The New Yorker, The Chicago Sun Times, The Boston Phoenix as well as other publications nationwide. These recordings showcase Brennans ability “to not just tip toes into genres, he strips naked and plunges right in.” Bill Holmes, AMPLIFIER.

events.title: Free Comedy Open-Mic
events.description:
18+ w/ Suggested 2-Item Minimum. Free Show. No Ticket Needed. 
The Globe described the night as “Comedy Hell” when we were upstairs in Somerville’s Union Square. Now we’re two floors down in a basement in Cambridge’s Harvard Square, under where the first and last Curious George shop died. It’s even better. Enter our comedy world, whether you listen, laugh, bomb, groan, clap or just sip your beverage with a raised eyebrow… We just LOVE it. It’s an Open Mic, but there’s always some pros dropping in to test new material, so it’s unavoidable to have a good time, for free no less. Doors open at 6 pm. The heat starts around 7 pm.

events.description:
All Ages w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. 
Live Dungeons & Dragons with comedians! Tess Dion, the game master, will guide comedians through a one-shot. This one-shot takes place in Cambridge, that is home to a hidden realm of magic, where the extraordinary coexists with the ordinary. Come and experience this live game, and perhaps, give (polite or funny) advice or just enjoy the afternoon watching the highs, lows and see how they avoid certain death with a beverage and snack in your hand! Disclaimer: Please note that, while this is mostly a kid-friendly show, there will be some swearing and other PG-13 content.

events.title: Dylan Block
events.description: Originally from Sacramento, CA, Dylan Block is an acoustic guitarist known for his soulful songwriting. His music blends heartfelt lyrics with melodic arrangements to create a unique and engaging listener experience. In addition to his original music, Dylan enjoys performing unique renditions of songs from the 60s all the way through the early 2000s.

events.title: CoastWise Radio
events.description: CoastWise Radio is a Somerville-based, 6 piece acoustic band featuring fiddle, mandolin, guitars, bass and vocals. We do a range of music from traditional/bluegrass, to classic country to contemporary Americana.

events.title: Orkest
events.description:
Founded in 2022 by Sapphire Skye Toth and Michael Gehman while they earned their Master of Science degrees in Arts Administration at Boston University, Orkest is meant to be the place where New Music meets The Community and The Community meets New Music.

Orkest, a visionary musical venture rooted in the bustling city of Boston, is on a mission to redefine the local music scene. Recognizing the abundance of untapped musical talent, Orkest seeks to bridge the gap between emerging composers and the community, breaking away from classical traditions and creating an inviting space for diverse genres. Departing from conventional models, Orkest utilizes venues to bring people to music, not just vice versa, fostering a casual environment in cafe bars and libraries. With a commitment to equitable treatment of artists, creating opportunities for artists at socioeconomic disadvantages, professional recordings of performances, and a unique pay-what-you-wish model, Orkest is not just a music experience; it's a transformative force shaping how communities engage with and appreciate new music and the artists who create it.

events.title: Molly McKeon
events.description: Molly McKeon is a New England-based Americana and folk musician whose sound is rooted in bluegrass, country, and acoustic storytelling. A fiddle player since childhood and a self-taught guitarist, she performs a blend of original music and carefully curated covers with warm vocals and an authentic, down-to-earth style. No matter the venue, Molly creates an inviting atmosphere that connects with audiences of all ages.

events.title: TCS presents: Sean Patton
events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum.
Sean Patton
 is a comedian based in New York, by way of New Orleans. He began doing stand-up in the Crescent City and has since performed in comedy clubs and festivals across the world, including The Melbourne International Comedy Festival (2011, 2018), The Glasgow Comedy Festival (2016), the Dublin Comedy fest (2017), and The Edinburgh Fringe (2017, 2019). Other festival appearances include SXSW (2016, 2017, 2018, 2022), The Moontower Comedy Festival (2012, 2013, 2014, 2019), Just for Laughs Chicago (2013), Just for Laughs Toronto (2013), and Just for Laughs Montreal (2008, 2010, 2012, 2016, 2018). He's performed on Comedy Central’s 
Live at Gotham
 (2009), 
Late Night with Jimmy Fallon
 (2010), 
Conan
 (2011, 2013), and Comedy Central’s 
The Half Hour 
(2013). More recently, He's been on 
@midnight
 (2014, 2015), 
The Meltdown with Jonah and Kumail 
(2015), 
This Is Not Happening
 (2015, 2017, 2019), Showtime's 
Live from SXSW 
(2017), TruTv's 
Comedy Knockout
 (2016, 2017, 2018), and 
This week at the Comedy Cellar 
(2018, 2019, 2020). In 2019, he released his second album, "
Scuttlebutt", 
on Helium Records. His next album 
King Scorpio, 
was released on Christmas Eve 2021 and as a special on YouTube in August 2022. His special 
Number One 
streamed on Peacock from 2022 to 2024 and is currently available on 800lb Gorilla’s YouTube page. As for acting, He's appeared on IFC's 
Maron, 
Comedy Central's 
Inside Amy Schumer, 
and TruTV's 
Those Who Can't. 
He stars alongside Brian Jordan Alvarez, Stephanie Koenig, Enrico Colantoni, and Carmen Christopher in two seasons of FX’s 
English Teacher

past_events.title: Gill Aharon Trio
past_events.description:
$10 / 8:15 start / 8pm doors / Seated Show 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Inspired by the cartoon music of the 50s and 60s, the drive of Led Zeppelin, the genius and humor of Frank Zappa, the perfection of Bach, the relentlessness of Ligeti, the courage of Medeski, Martin and Wood, the jam of Phish live, the beauty of the Beatles and the immersive nature of music, I am in pursuit of truth through music. “Sometimes I think of us musicians as kids playing in order to learn how to be adults, but we can just keep playing. The game of playing music together is too much fun to make it work. With that in mind, I refuse to write arrangements. I never want my friends and bandmates to feel obliged to do anything. - Gill Aharon Gill Aharon , Piano Jef Charland , Bass Andrew Stern , Guitar Randy Wooten , Drums - Continuing Every Wednesday  Lineup subject to change gillaharontrio.com

past_events.title: Sophie Wellington Album Release + Square Dance!
past_events.description:
$20 admission / 7:30 doors / 8pm start / seated & standing Celebrate the release of Sophie's new album, Her Bright Smile Haunts Me Still with a intimate and guitar forward solo set, followed by a celebratory square dance! 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Her Bright Smile Haunts Me Still is Sophie's second album, and it focuses on how to approach playing guitar like a fiddler, singer, and percussive dancer. She uses alternate guitar tunings and "fiddle picking" techniques to emulate the feel and sound of the fiddle. She accompanies her voice using harmonic gravity, rhythmic velocity, and her experience as a ballad singer and jazz vocalist. HBSHMS is artfully recorded, mixed, and produced by Grammy Award winning artist Cathy Fink. Live tracked and performed solo, it is a souvenir of Sophie's dynamic musicianship. Sophie Wellington is an old time fiddler, flatpick guitarist, percussive dancer and jazz vocalist based in Boston, MA. Sophie grew up in Staunton, VA and was raised in its vibrant music community by musician and dance caller Bill Wellington and classical pianist Lynne Mackey. Sophie graduated from Berklee College of Music in 2022 with a degree in jazz voice and American roots music, and has since taught fiddle, flatfooting, guitar, and music theory at numerous festivals and camps. Sophie also toured extensively with musician and modern day bard Willi Carlisle. website bandcamp album pre-save

past_events.title: h2 Live @ The Lilypad
past_events.description:
$10 cover / 10pm doors / 10:30 start / seated & standing h2 , the fusion project of violinist and composer Luke Norman, plays original compositions and arrangements from their debut EP, “Running Late”. The performance features Luke Norman on violin, Angelo Della Valle on guitar, Anderson Mirafzali on bass, and Nick White on drums. 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 h2 is a Boston-based fusion artist whose music bridges the worlds of jazz, electronic, and experimental rock. His music combines  intricate melodies with high-octane grooves that exemplify precision, depth, and a forward-thinking approach to fusion. Drawing inspiration from Mahavishnu Orchestra, Ghost Note, and Machine Girl, his music bridges the analog warmth of classic fusion with the freneticism  of contemporary electronic music.

past_events.title: Hatha Yoga w/ Live Music
past_events.description:
$20-$25 at the door / $15 students & seniors / 12pm start / Level 1 Experience the magic of a moving meditation with Integral Yoga instructor Vienna-Parashakti. This 90-minute class is appropriate for all students, from beginners to advanced yogis. Dress comfortably, and bring a cozy blanket if you’d like. 
 
 
 
 
    Reserve your spot
   
 
 
 
 
 
 (cash or venmo accepted at the door) 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Prepare to relax and rejuvenate your body, mind, and spirit in this peaceful yoga class. Explore the guided practice of Hatha Yoga and experience a series of classical Yoga asanas (physical postures), pranayama (breath control), Yoga Nidra (deep relaxation) and meditation.  
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Parashakti  (Vienna) is an Integral Yoga instructor and a new-ish resident to Cambridge from two and a half years studying and teaching at  Satchidananda Ashram  in Yogaville, Virginia.  She is currently teaching weekly Hatha Yoga classes at the Lilypad and other places around Cambridge. 
 
 
 
 
 
 Musical accompaniment on the Grand Piano provided by Jesse Gallagher, along with selections from his meditation album "The Inner Sound." 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 “ It was such a delight to attend your class.  My nervous system has been a wreck and it was wonderful to be in a place that felt safe for my body.  I haven’t moved my “yogi” body in years and it felt so good to wake up the cobwebs. Thank you for providing such a beautiful space for me in a day that I really needed it. ” 
 
 — Maria 
 
 
 
 
 Yoga Classes at Lilypad continue weekly on Saturdays.

past_events.description: Toad Revival Series — Saturdays, 4–7 PM  Every Saturday from 4 to 7, we rewind the clock and let the old sparks fly. The  Toad Revival Series  is our tribute to the bands that built this stage, note by note, night after night. It’s a laid-back, early evening show filled with familiar sounds, longtime favorites, and the spirit of a scene that never really faded—just waited for the right time to plug back in. These are the artists who gave Toad its heartbeat, returning for intimate, no-frills sets that remind us why we’re here. Think front-porch energy with barroom volume, old songs with new life, and a crowd that knows when to sing along. Come early. Stay as long as you like. This is Toad, the way it was—and still is. Electric Heaters -  www.heatonsurf.com

past_events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.
Rory James
 is a Boston-born comedian who has been doing stand-up in Los Angeles for over 10 years and has a strong social media presence. His debut special Rory James: Acquired Taste was just released to streaming by major label Comedy Dynamics on 
Apple TV, Amazon Prime, Spotify, Apple Music,
 and more major streamers. Now, he's bringing it all home to celebrate his new special in his hometown with brand new material. In Acquired Taste, Rory bares all with a delightfully raunchy account of his misadventures in dating dudes, sex, and medical offices. You can take the kid out of Boston but you can't take the Boston out of the kid, as Rory can't seem to stay out of trouble as he navigates crushes, hook-ups, and his own personality.

past_events.title: TCS presents: Toni Nagy: That Was Not a Microdose
past_events.description:
18+ w/ 2-Item Suggested Minimum. Please Arrive Early to Guarantee Seating.




Toni Nagy’s comedy is NOT a microdose. Come macrodose Toni’s reality in this psychedelic stand up show. This promises to be a comedy adventure for anyone who is currently waking up in an existential crisis while releasing primal guttural screams at the state of the world. If you are looking to connect to others whose eyes are also “Clockwork Orange-style” peeled open while doom scrolling, and you feel the yearning to emotionally moisturize into a new vision of society - then this is the show for you!!!!  


This comedy show will provide comedic relief to help release the emotional constipation of this current caca-fest. Toni’s stand up self-reflects in a fun house of mirrors while bumping into cultural reflections. Her work is aimed to dismantle our societal scaffolding so we can build ourselves back up together as a community. The audience will collectively come-in-unity at Toni's comedy, that is a guarantee.


When you macrosose Toni, you deprogram yourself from the deep state by gyrating the collective consciousness of our crotches into a new paradigm. Toni peels off the skin of her mask to reveal the true face of her own brain washing. She controls her mind to examine the mind control that has controlled her. This comedy show will be the emotional salve you need to heal the wounds of our mutual traumas. Also, Elon Musk once tweeted that Toni “seems dumb” because of her political beliefs - which is Toni’s greatest accomplishment.  Go to 
toninagy.com
 to find out more about her

past_events.title: Luther Gray
past_events.description:
$15 admission / $10 students / 7pm start / seated 
 
 
 
 Drummer  Luther Gray , though largely self-taught, his musical training involved drum lessons from Kim Martin, Larry Bright, Steve Bagby, and Mickey Newman. In 1995 he graduated the University of Miami with a Bachelor of Music, after which he taught privately and performed in the Washington , D.C. area with, among others, Butch Warren, Cecil Payne, Webster Young, Tsunami, Liquorice, Peter Edelman, Jenny Toomey, Bob Butta, and Buck Hill. Since moving to Boston he has resumed teaching private drum lessons and has played with Joe Morris, Jay Hoggard, Joe McPhee, Timo Shanko, Cameron Brown, Allan Chase, Joseph Daley, Sabir Mateen, Roy Campbell, Ida, Geoff Farina, Andrew White, Rob Brown, Bill Lowe, Greg Abate, Raqib Hassan, Bill Pierce, Mitch Seidman, Steve Swell, Joe Beck, Fred Anderson, Ken Vandermark and many others. In addition to his performing schedule Luther teaches art and music at an after-school program for elementary school children.

past_events.title: Jerry Bergonzi Quartet
past_events.description:
$15 @ the door / $10 students / 8:30 start / 8pm doors / seated  Jerry Bergonzi , Tenor Saxophone Luther Gray , Drums Special Guest , Trumpet Special Guest , Bass 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 A fine, high-powered tenor saxophonist with a tone influenced by John Coltrane, a mastery of chord changes, and a strong musical imagination,  Jerry Bergonzi  has long had an underground following in the Boston area. He started on clarinet when he was eight, switching to alto at 12, and finally to tenor two years later. Bergonzi was inspired early on by Sonny Rollins, Coltrane, and Hank Mobley. He attended Lowell University and then after graduation played electric bass in local bands behind singers and strippers, saving up enough money to move to New York in 1972. After struggling in the Big Apple for seven years and gaining some recognition as a member of Two Generations of Brubeck and of the Dave Brubeck Quartet (with whom he appeared on several Concord albums during 1979-1981), Bergonzi moved back to Boston in 1981, where he developed a strong career both as a tenorman and as an educator. He has since led several groups (including two called Con Brio and Gonz) and recorded for the Plug, Not Fat, Red, and Blue Note labels. —  By Scott Yanow, AllMusic 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Continuing Every Monday at 8:30pm Lineup subject to change
